title: Achoriphragma runcinatum
taxonomy: Achoriphragma runcinatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a type of thistle native to the Mediterranean region, specifically in countries such as Greece and Turkey. The plant belongs to the tribe Cardueae within the aster family. Further classification of Achoriphragma runcinatum places it in the subtribe Carduinae. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered a member of the aster family.
uses: Unfortunately, I could not find any information about Achoriphragma runcinatum's medicinal uses.
constituents: Achoriphragma runcinatum contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The exact composition and concentration of these active constituents may vary depending on the source and preparation of the plant. Research suggests that flavonoids in Achoriphragma runcinatum have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Alkaloids present in the plant may exhibit antimicrobial and antispasmodic activities. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the pharmacological properties of Achoriphragma runcinatum and its potential applications.
